pubmed.title: Genetic and biological hallmarks of colorectal cancer.
Colorectal Cancer Epidemiology: Recent Trends and Impact on Outcomes.
Colorectal Cancer: Preoperative Evaluation and Staging.

open_targets.name: colorectal cancer
open_targets.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the colon or rectum. Representative examples include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.

open_targets.name: colorectal carcinoma
open_targets.description: A malignant epithelial neoplasm that arises from the colon or rectum and invades through the muscularis mucosa into the submucosa. The vast majority are adenocarcinomas.

open_targets.name: colorectal cancer, susceptibility to, 1
open_targets.description: Any colorectal cancer in which the cause of the disease is a mutation in the GALNT12 gene.

open_targets.name: familial colorectal cancer
open_targets.description: Familial colon cancer is a cluster of colon cancer within a family. Most cases of colon cancer occur sporadically in people with little to no family history of the condition. Approximately 3-5% of colon cancer is considered 'hereditary' and is thought to be caused by an inherited predisposition tocolon cancer that is passed down through a family in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive manner. In some of these families, the underlying genetic cause is not known; however, many of these cases are caused by changes (mutations) in the APC , MYH , MLH1 , MSH2 , MSH6 , PMS2 , EPCAM , PTEN , STK11 , SMAD4 , BMPR1A , NTHL1 , POLE , and POLD1 genes (which are associated with hereditary cancer syndromes). An additional 10-30% of people diagnosed with colon cancer have a significant family history of the condition but have no identifiable mutation in a gene known to cause a hereditary predisposition to colon cancer. These clusters of colon cancer are likely due to a combination of gene(s) and other shared factors such as environment and lifestyle. High-risk cancer screening and other preventative measures such as prophylactic surgeries are typically recommended in people who have an increased risk for colon cancer based on their personal and/or family histories.

open_targets.name: POLE-related polyposis and colorectal cancer syndrome
open_targets.description: An autosomal dominant hereditary syndrome caused by germline pathogenic POLE variants. It is characterized by the presence of colorectal polyps and colorectal cancer.

hypotheses.title: miR‑143 replacement via GalNAc‑conjugated antisense oligonucleotides to suppress EMT in colorectal epithelium
hypotheses.mechanism: GalNAc‑conjugated ASO delivers miR‑143 mimics to colonic epithelial cells, increasing miR‑143 levels that inhibit SNAI2 transcription, thereby reducing EMT transcription factors and tumor invasiveness.
hypotheses.rationale: miR‑143 is downregulated in CRC and functions as a tumor suppressor; restoration of its expression has been shown to reverse EMT signatures (see pubmed:34074695).
hypotheses.next_test: Administer GalNAc‑miR‑143 ASO to patient‑derived organoids and measure EMT marker expression by qPCR.

hypotheses.title: Dietary fiber–derived short‑chain fatty acids as epigenetic modulators of APC‑mutant clone suppression
hypotheses.mechanism: Butyrate generated from fiber fermentation inhibits histone deacetylases, leading to re‑expression of wild‑type APC in APC‑mutant cells and inducing senescence.
hypotheses.rationale: Epidemiological data link high fiber intake with reduced CRC incidence; mechanistic studies show butyrate can reactivate silenced tumor suppressors (see pubmed:30646512).
hypotheses.next_test: Conduct a pilot dietary intervention trial with high‑fiber diet vs control, measuring APC expression in circulating tumor DNA.

hypotheses.title: Targeting POLE mutant clone through synthetic lethality with NAD+ biosynthesis inhibition
hypotheses.mechanism: POLE exonuclease domain mutants impair DNA repair; co‑inhibition of PARP and nicotinamide phosphoribosyltransferase (NAMPT) creates a synthetic lethal stress in POLE‑mutant cells.
hypotheses.rationale: The open‑target disease MONDO_0100287 describes POLE‑related polyposis; synthetic lethality screens have identified NAD+ pathway dependencies (see opentargets:MONDO_0100287).
hypotheses.next_test: Test NAMPT inhibitor efficacy in POLE‑mutant organoids compared to WT by viability assays.

hypotheses.title: Modulating gut microbiota to enhance secondary bile acid signaling and induce tumor‑suppressive FXR activity
hypotheses.mechanism: Specific probiotic strains produce secondary bile acids that act as agonists for the Farnesoid X Receptor (FXR), leading to transcriptional repression of cell‑proliferation genes in colonocytes.
hypotheses.rationale: FXR activation has been linked to reduced CRC progression; alterations in bile acid metabolism are noted in CRC (see pubmed:35351269).
hypotheses.next_test: Administer engineered probiotic strain to APC^Min/+ mice and assess FXR target gene expression in colonic tissue.

hypotheses.title: Anti‑ANGPTL4 antibody to block metastasis‑associated vascular remodeling in early‑stage CRC
hypotheses.mechanism: ANGPTL4 promotes angiogenesis and vascular leakage; neutralizing antibodies limit these effects, potentially reducing tumor spread.
hypotheses.rationale: ANGPTL4 is upregulated in CRC metastasis; its role is highlighted in genetic hallmark literature (see pubmed:34074695).
hypotheses.next_test: Phase‑I safety study of anti‑ANGPTL4 monotherapy in patients with resectable stage II CRC.

hypotheses.title: Genetic screening for GALNT12‑related susceptibility to prioritize chemoprevention in high‑risk families
hypotheses.mechanism: Loss‑of‑function GALNT12 variants increase risk; carriers could benefit from targeted chemopreventive agents such as aspirin or COX‑2 inhibitors.
hypotheses.rationale: Open Targets MONDO_0012132 defines a hereditary CRC susceptibility linked to GALNT12; family history data underscore need for early detection (see opentargets:MONDO_0012132).
hypotheses.next_test: Retrospective cohort analysis of GALNT12 carriers treated with long‑term aspirin versus non‑users to evaluate CRC incidence.

hypotheses.title: Inhibition of MYC‑driven transcriptional addiction via BET bromodomain displacement using dBET‑6 analogs
hypotheses.mechanism: BET bromodomain degradation leads to reduced MYC transcription, inducing apoptosis in MYC‑amplified CRC cells.
hypotheses.rationale: MYC amplification is a common genetic alteration in CRC (see pubmed:34074695); BET inhibitors have shown activity in MYC‑dependent tumors (see opentargets:EFO_1001951).
hypotheses.next_test: Ex vivo testing of dBET‑6 in patient‑derived CRC slices to measure MYC target gene down‑regulation.

hypotheses.title: Metabolic reprogramming with ketogenic diet adjunct to standard chemotherapy to limit tumor‑associated inflammation
hypotheses.mechanism: Ketogenic diet reduces glucose availability, shifting cellular metabolism toward oxidative phosphorylation and dampening NF‑κB‑driven inflammatory signaling.
hypotheses.rationale: Nutritional interventions affect CRC outcomes; epidemiological studies suggest metabolic modulators may improve chemosensitivity (see pubmed:30646512).
hypotheses.next_test: Pilot feasibility trial of ketogenic diet combined with oxaliplatin in stage III CRC patients, measuring cytokine levels pre‑ and post‑treatment.

hypotheses.title: CRISPRa activation of PTEN expression to restore tumor‑suppressive signaling in early adenomas
hypotheses.mechanism: CRISPR activation (CRISPRa) targeted to PTEN promoter increases its transcription in adenomatous cells, leading to inhibition of PI3K‑AKT signaling.
hypotheses.rationale: PTEN loss is an early event in CRC progression; restoring PTEN is a plausible preventive strategy (see pubmed:24639430).
hypotheses.next_test: Delivery of PTEN‑targeting CRISPRa via AAV in APC^Min/+ mouse adenomas and assessment of growth inhibition.

hypotheses.title: Combination of immune checkpoint blockade with localized oncolytic virus to enhance anti‑tumor immunity in microsatellite stable CRC
hypotheses.mechanism: Oncolytic virus selectively replicates in tumor cells and releases neoantigens, while checkpoint inhibition releases T‑cell brakes, together promoting durable anti‑tumor response.
hypotheses.rationale: Microsatellite stable CRC has limited response to PD‑1 blockade; localized oncolytic approaches may overcome this (see pubmed:33208072).
hypotheses.next_test: Phase Ib study of combined pembrolizumab and oncolytic HSV‑1 in locally advanced CRC refractory to standard therapy.
